content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (like ICM and RND for autonomous agents) can be repurposed to dynamically prioritize which new memories an active learning agent retains and revisits, creating a self-sustaining active memory system that outperforms 2024 SOTA static memory pruning methods. This curiosity-based memory selection naturally aligns with an agent's ongoing learning goals, retaining only high-value, uncertainty-reducing memories that drive future performance gains.
